titleStmt.title: Despatch from Owen L. W. Smith to David J. Hill, October 26, 1900

distributor.name: Center for Digital Research in the Humanities

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported License. Derivatives must be credited to To Enter Africa from America, made available non-commercially, and distributed under the same terms. Requests for permission for commercial publication or other use should be emailed to the project team.

bibl.title: Despatch from Owen L. W. Smith to David J. Hill, October 26, 1900
Despatches from United States Consuls in Monrovia, 1852-1906

correspAction.roleName: U.S. Minister Resident and Consul General

div1.note: Index Bureau Dept of State Rec'd. Dec 8 '00
div1.p:
No. 51. Consular.
Legation of the United States.
Monrovia Liberia,
Oct. 26, 1900.

Hon. David J Hill,
Assistant Secretary of State,
Washington.
Receipt of correspondence, relative to the Estate of the late Auranus M. Miller, at Harper, Maryland Co. Liberia

No. 51. Consular.
Legation of the United States,
Monrovia Liberia,
Oct. 26 1900.

Hon. David J. Hill,
Assistant Secretary of State,
Washington.

I have to acknowledge the receipt, Oct. 25, 1900, of your No. 53, Sept. 26, 1900, Dip Series, with 5 enclosures, relative to the estate of the late Auranus M. Miller, deceased March 1890, at Harper, (Cape Palmas) Maryland County Liberia.

In reply I have the honor to say that the records and files of this Legation are being searched as fast as possible, for any an all correspondence, between this Legation and the State and other Departments
Departments, here; the widow and other heirs, at law, in America, as well as other parties consulted with said correspondence, here, and elsewhere. In connection, herewith, I have to inform you that R. H. Gibson, whose letter of August 21, 1891, to Mrs. E. A. Miller, was an enclosure, died at Harper, (Cape Palmas) May 28, 1900.

I have the honor to be
your obedient servant; Owen L. W. Smith
M. R. and C. General.
